Growlithe

#0057 Primeape #0059 Arcanine
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Growlithe is a Fire type Pokémon introduced in Generation 1.

Growlithe has a Hisuian Form, introduced in Pokémon Legends: Arceus.

Growlithe Hisuian Growlithe

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0058
Type Fire
Species Puppy Pokémon
Height 0.7 m (2′04″)
Weight 19.0 kg (41.9 lbs)
Abilities 1. Intimidate
2. Flash Fire
Justified (hidden ability)
Local № 0058 (Red/Blue/Yellow)
0127 (Gold/Silver/Crystal)
0058 (FireRed/LeafGreen)
0129 (HeartGold/SoulSilver)
0051 (Black 2/White 2)
0052 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0064 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0058 (Let's Go Pikachu/Let's Go Eevee)
0070 (Sword/Shield)
0150 (Legends: Arceus)
0213 (Scarlet/Violet)
0078 (The Teal Mask)

Training

EV yield 1 Attack
Catch rate 190 (24.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 70
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Field
Gender 75% male, 25% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Evolution chart

#0058
Growlithe
Fire
(use Fire Stone)
#0059
Arcanine
Fire
#0058
Growlithe
Hisuian Growlithe
Fire · Rock
(use Fire Stone)
#0059
Arcanine
Hisuian Arcanine
Fire · Rock

Growlithe changes

  • In Generation 8, Hisuian Growlithe has Justified as its hidden ability.
  • In Generation 1, Growlithe has a base Special stat of 50.
  • In Generations 1-4, Growlithe has a base experience yield of 91.
  • In Generations 2-7, Growlithe has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Growlithe

Red
Blue		 Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.
Yellow A POKéMON with a friendly nature. However, it will bark fiercely at anything invading its territory.
Gold It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
Silver Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own trainer from harm.
Crystal It controls a big territory. If it detects an unknown smell, it roars loudly to force out the intruder.
Ruby
Sapphire		 GROWLITHE has a superb sense of smell. Once it smells anything, this POKéMON won’t forget the scent, no matter what. It uses its advanced olfactory sense to determine the emotions of other living things.
FireRed Very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.
LeafGreen It is very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.
Emerald Its superb sense of smell ensures that this POKéMON won’t forget any scent, no matter what. It uses its sense of smell to detect the emotions of others.
Diamond
Pearl
Platinum		 A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.
HeartGold It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
SoulSilver Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.
Black
White		 A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.
Black 2
White 2		 Extremely loyal to its Trainer, it will bark at those who approach the Trainer unexpectedly and run them out of town.
X Very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.
Y It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
Omega Ruby
Alpha Sapphire		 Growlithe has a superb sense of smell. Once it smells anything, this Pokémon won’t forget the scent, no matter what. It uses its advanced olfactory sense to determine the emotions of other living things.
Sun It’s both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.
Moon It looks cute, but when you approach another Trainer’s Growlithe, it will bark at you and bite.
Ultra Sun While it’s quite friendly toward humans once it’s grown used to them, in the wild it must be quite fierce to defend its territory from Rockruff.
Ultra Moon It has lived alongside humans since ages ago. Its bones have been found in excavations of ruins from the Stone Age.
Let's Go Pikachu
Let's Go Eevee		 A Pokémon with a friendly nature. However, it will bark fiercely at anything invading its territory.
Sword It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
Shield Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 It is a Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.
Scarlet It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
Violet It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Hisuian Growlithe

Legends: Arceus They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Growlithe does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Growlithe cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Answers to Growlithe questions

  • If you can only catch a Growlithe in HeartGold, what can you only catch in SoulSilver?
  • Can I catch Vulpix in White 2?
  • Why is Growlithe the 'puppy Pokemon'?
  • How do I get my Arcanine to know Flamethrower and Extremespeed in Gold?
  • What is a good LC moveset for Growlithe?
  • Why is 600 so special for pseudo-legendaries?
  • How do you get Seadra and Growlithe to evolve in Pokemon Gold?
  • Why is Espeon not passing down Morning Sun onto Growlithe?
  • Can I get a torchic on soul silver or can I catch a growlithe?
  • Where can I catch Growlithe in Pokemon Emerald?

  • View more questions on PokéBase »

Name origin

growl
gruff noise made by dogs
lithe
gracefully slender
